headlines.title: Mahmood announces new refugee sponsorship route into UK
headlines.description: The home secretary says "capped safe and legal" routes would help to restore confidence in the asylum system.

headlines.title: Are we in for a summer of serial heatwaves?
headlines.description: Forecasters have suggested that summer is likely to be warmer than average with an increased chance of more heatwaves, as Simon King explains.

headlines.title: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
headlines.description: Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response.

headlines.title: Meloni and Trump: A very public fall-out that is proving very hard to fix
headlines.description: Italy's PM was not long ago being called the "Trump whisperer", but their relationship has gone from public attacks to personal insults.

headlines.title: Is Andy Burnham Labour's saviour, or just its best bet?
headlines.description: Labour insiders give Laura Kuenssberg their take on the man tipped to replace Keir Starmer as PM.

headlines.title: Madonna was 'jealous of Kylie' - and more things we learned in her Graham Norton interview
headlines.description: All the revelations from Madonna's chat with Graham Norton, including a big hint about Glastonbury.

headlines.title: How Cape Verde stunned World Cup to set up Argentina tie
headlines.description: How has a group of islands with just 525,000 inhabitants made it to the knockout rounds of the World Cup?

headlines.title: How completing the World Cup sticker book is like having a second job
headlines.description: From car park meet ups to Facebook groups, how families try to complete the set on a budget.

headlines.title: See it, say it, not sorted. I was let down when I reported my train sexual assault
headlines.description: After reporting an incident on a train, Esme Rice waited 13 hours for a response from the British Transport Police.

headlines.title: Two years ago, she was delivering parcels - now KWN is an R&B star
headlines.description: The R&B singer broke out with the single Worst Behaviour, which she sold on a custom-built website.

headlines.title: They quit the West for Russia's traditional values. It wasn't what they expected
headlines.description: The BBC asks Westerners who moved to Russia if life there lives up to expectations.

headlines.title: Going to a festival as a neurodivergent person can be tough - but there are ways to recharge
headlines.description: Some festivals are rolling out calm spaces and noise-cancelling headphones, but there are calls to go further.

headlines.title: Why GTA 6 will launch without a disc and what it means for gamers
headlines.description: Music and films are now largely digital-only - does the lack of a disc for GTA 6 mean gaming is going the same way?

headlines.title: Three unusual things about the King's tax bill
headlines.description: King Charles paid £12.9m in tax for 2024-2025 - here's what we know about his unique tax situation.

headlines.title: Could you handle a 20-plus hour flight? This airline is banking on it
headlines.description: Some analysts say the first non-stop London to Sydney flight is a major milestone - but would you buy a ticket?

headlines.title: Harry and Meghan accept offer to stay at royal estate during UK visit
headlines.description: The royal residence the pair will use has not been made public, but they will also be staying in private accommodation during the trip.

headlines.title: How messages between two dads helped expose the largest NHS maternity scandal
headlines.description: Among the hundreds of affected families in Nottingham, two sets of parents led the fight to get answers.

headlines.title: A smartphone ban in English schools is coming into force. What's changing?
headlines.description: Schools must now follow government guidance that says schools should be phone-free for the entire day.

headlines.title: Unwanted items from Alan Carr's castle head to auction
headlines.description: The sale includes dozens of items from a "concrete menagerie" of animals, figures and rural scenes.

headlines.title: We host strangers for dinner every month - now we have 60 new friends
headlines.description: After 2 years in Belfast, Nidhi Pal and Vik Parashar found a creative way to make new friends - a monthly dinner party with strangers.

headlines.title: I'm 98 but I still do my 40 press-ups every day
headlines.description: Bill Kober, who lives in Woodbridge, is proving that age is just a number... one press-up at a time.

headlines.title: Banned swimmer's daughters to realise family's Commonwealth Games dream
headlines.description: Annette Cowley was not allowed to compete at the 1986 Games as she was caught in a political storm.

headlines.title: Rescued sewage work eggs hatch into five ducklings
headlines.description: The eggs were spotted in a nest at risk of toppling over on the surface at a sewage treatment works.

headlines.title: Weekend to bring cooldown after record-breaking heatwave
headlines.description:
The record-breaking heatwave is set to end with cooler and more unsettled weather by 
Sunday.

headlines.title: Hottest June day record broken for third day in row as temperature hits 37.3C
headlines.description: Suffolk saw the highest temperature on Friday - but many will feel the heatwave begin to ease over the weekend.

headlines.title: Watch: Lightning strikes seen across UK
headlines.description: Lightning strikes lit up the sky across the UK during the early hours of Friday as thunderstorms were triggered by the intense heat.

headlines.title: Gripping, star-studded environmental thriller

headlines.description: With Uruguay eliminated from the 2026 World Cup, BBC Sport looks at what has gone wrong for them under colourful coach Marcelo Bielsa.

headlines.title: George criticises 'unacceptable' Auvaa over nightclub incident
